title: Day 1
text_excerpt: Day 1 Oops! Sorry!! This site doesn't support Internet Explorer. Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Firefox or Edge. Language Of The Heart Intro Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Day 6 Day 7 Day 8 Privacy Policy Paul Cropper Home EXERCISE 1 Part 1 of the Story of Iron John Stories, symbols, metaphors, emotions... these are the language of the heart.   As you allow yourselves to listen and enjoy a story, the heart is able to hear it. 1- Listen to the first part of the story of Iron John for 3 days (try for consecutive). First thing in the morning, or before bed is a good time to access the Heart. 2- "Heart-storming"...  How do you FEEL about the story? What stands out to YOU? What does it bring to mind (even if seemingly unrelated)?. Take out a notebook, set a timer for 5-10 minutes, and write anything that comes to mind. (If on the go, make a voice memo, and record thoughts aloud.) title: Day 2
text_excerpt: Day 2 Oops! Sorry!! This site doesn't support Internet Explorer. Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Firefox or Edge. Language Of The Heart Intro Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Day 6 Day 7 Day 8 Privacy Policy Paul Cropper Home Day 2 The cage I build, is all the stories and beliefs that keep me from being my authentic self.  They are the things that keep me from growing and expanding. Watch the Video, then see the PDF for the emotional fitness training. Heart Exercise  2 If you like to go deep, you can spend 4 consecutive days on this exercise. You can go back to portions of the recording as desired.  Set a Timer to 5-10 minutes. Ask the questions and write what comes to mind.   Ask your heart as if it is REAL, and can answer....    Directing the question to the heart, opens a path for information to flow between the Heart-Body and the Brain..   (Day 1) What is one (more more) way(s) you were told you "should" be?  (Day 2) What is one (or more) way you were told you "should" behave?  (Day 3) What is one way you are you telling yourself you "should" be?  (Day 4) What is one thing you lost?  (i.e. a piece of your personality, or something that made you happy...) title: Day 3
text_excerpt: Day 3 Oops! Sorry!! This site doesn't support Internet Explorer. Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Firefox or Edge. Language Of The Heart Intro Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Day 6 Day 7 Day 8 Privacy Policy Paul Cropper Home Day 3 Non-Judgement, practiced with honesty, is KEY to emotional freedom. Watch the Video, then see the PDF for the emotional fitness training. Heart Exercise 3 Spend some time with the following questions... You can spend 5 minutes brainstorming per question, all at once, or split over 4  consecutive days.   Try using a 5 minute timer. For brainstorming, touch your Heart, address your Heart, and even if you are just looking at a blank page, spend time asking and considering the question.  (1) What does it mean to practice Non-Judgement? (2) Why is it important to practice Non-Judgement? (3) What is one mistake you have made? (4) And looking deeper into the mistake... can you find something innately good, some gold? title: Day 4
text_excerpt: Day 4 Oops! Sorry!! This site doesn't support Internet Explorer. Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Firefox or Edge. Language Of The Heart Intro Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Day 6 Day 7 Day 8 Privacy Policy Paul Cropper Home Day 4 Box Breathing- As a tool to Change, Shift, and Heal The Heart Heart Exercise # 4   Choose one word that you want to breathe in... and connect it to your breath.  Maybe it's love, or light, or courage, or inspiration. Choose your "word" or set of words. "Life" works well for many.  For example:   1- Inhale (4 counts, "Breathe in with Life")  2 - Hold Inhale (4 counts) 3 - Exhale (4 counts, "Breathe out with Life") 4 - Hold Exhale (4 counts)   To get the practice in your memory, so it's there when you need it most:  Practice box breathing- 8 cycles or 2 minutes, once a day for a week or month.  Try setting a daily reminder on your calendar, for the next several days at least.    Box breathing is a breath workout, an exercise, use it when you need it, not necessarily all the time.   Note on breathing for extended periods:   For continual, daily, long term breathing, to reduce stress, and PLANT the beliefs you want- use a controlled long exhale, breathe in deep (all the way) for 3-5 seconds, then control the slow exhale about 7-8 seconds... ("Breathe in with life... breathe out with life...") Please donate to support Paul and more Heart tools like this one.  Than

title: Day 5
text_excerpt: Day 5 Oops! Sorry!! This site doesn't support Internet Explorer. Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Firefox or Edge. Language Of The Heart Intro Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Day 6 Day 7 Day 8 Privacy Policy Paul Cropper Home Day 5 How do we tend our Hearts?   Unhappiness, anxiety, and much of our internal negative experiences are because we have not learned and taken the time to tend our hearts... Heart Exercise # 5 Why is it important for you to realize that your heart exists? List at least one reason.   How can you benefit by tending your heart as if it were a spring, or a fire, or a garden? What is one thing you can do to tend your heart?    Additional questions you may consider: What are you allowing to fall in the Spring?  How are you feeding the Fire? What are you growing in your Garden?   Most important: What is one thing you want to plant in your inner Garden? Please donate to support Paul and more Heart tools like this one.  Thank you!!! title: Day 6
text_excerpt: Day 6 Oops! Sorry!! This site doesn't support Internet Explorer. Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Firefox or Edge. Language Of The Heart Intro Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Day 6 Day 7 Day 8 Privacy Policy Paul Cropper Home Day 6 The Earth and The Masculine Heart   Centuries of thought, passed down, may imprint and effect our thinking more than we know. Beliefs rooted in simple phrases such as "mother earth" may have led to some modern deeply seeded stereotypes of the Masculine and Feminine.    Our language may even drive disconnection between Men and their Hearts. Consider giving space in your mind/heart to the phrase "father earth"... Heart Exercise 6 The Earth and The Masculine Heart   Set a timer for 5-10 minutes, and do the meditation outlined in the video. Simply visualize the string of light between yourself and the center of the earth. Follow it up as you inhale, follow it down as you exhale.   Do this for 10 consecutive days to start instilling the effects.  Use this visualization as a way to ground yourself anytime. title: Day 7
text_excerpt: Day 7 Oops! Sorry!! This site doesn't support Internet Explorer. Please use a modern browser like Chrome, Firefox or Edge. Language Of The Heart Intro Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Day 6 Day 7 Day 8 Privacy Policy Paul Cropper Home Day 7 Finding the big beliefs in life that you may need to let go of, may not happen in this sitting... But start to ask the question. What do I need to let go of? (apologize for the audio issue... but the pond... :) Heart Exercise 7 Name 2 BIG things that you associate strongly with your identity. Singing was one of mine... what is one of yours?   The actual thing may not be negative at all (you're a good listener, kind, likeable, thrifty, productive or hard working...), but is there some shadow belief or behavior associated with it?  (e.g. Pride, Guilt, Shame, ect.)   Our greatest "gold" is often hidden behind our shadows... Our greatest shadow is all too often attached to our greatest gold... This is what makes it hard to see. Please donate to support Paul and more Heart tools like this one.   Thank you!!! title: Day 8

Language Of The Heart Intro Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Day 6 Day 7 Day 8 Paul Cropper Home Day 8  Part 2/2 of the Story of Iron John We left off, with the boy being sent to work in the garden... here is the rest of the story. Stories impressed on the heart, give us space to learn in a different way. Don't worry about the detail. Enjoy the listen. Enjoy the story. Heart Exercise 8 Listen and let yourself feel. What is one feeling that you felt from the story? What is one part that stood out to you?
